title: bee-reviewing becomes a user-invoked review session: SKILL.md + reviewing-reference rewrite
action: Rewrite bee-reviewing from an automatic post-execution chain stage into a USER-INVOKED review session per SPEC (per 565e68d0). Frontmatter description: replace 'Use when the final swarm slice completes, or when the user asks...' with user-request-only wording (R1). Opening framing: review is an independent inspection over a completed, immutable scope — like a second team receiving a PR/RC — never a completion trigger; verification (bee-executing/cap rules) remains the mandatory per-cell proof and is NOT this skill (R2, §5 status table: completed ≠ reviewed). Add sections: (1) TRIGGER — the explicit-intent list from R1 (review this / review today's work / review features A+B / review diff X..Y / review all unreviewed before release); a finished cell/slice/feature/day and the words merge/ship/release are NEVER triggers — for merge/ship/release report review status and ask ONE explicit question (7.4/A9; silence or a non-answer = no dispatch). (2) SCOPE RESOLUTION (R4) — the five boundary types; if the request does not pin a boundary ask exactly ONE boundary question, never a second permission question when scope is already clear; batch scopes resolve candidates via node .bee/bin/bee_reviews.mjs candidates + status, build ONE cumulative diff with a mapping from each diff region to its source feature/cell (7.3); in-progress (open/claimed) work is excluded with reason and stated (A6, 7.5); active feature state is preserved — never cap/drop/assume in-flight work complete (7.5). (3) SCOPE FREEZE + PREVIEW (R5) — before any reviewer dispatch: create the session via node .bee/bin/bee_reviews.mjs create --file <scope.json> (preflight fails closed on missing verification evidence, A10 — surface the error, never dispatch reviewers to compensate) then show the §9 preview: covered features/cells, baseline/head, exclusions, expected core+conditional reviewer count, review model/tier or external executor, and warnings for oversized scope; record the reviewer manifest via bee_reviews.mjs record --id <session-id> --kind manifest (record always needs --id). scope.json documentation note: top-level keys are in the CLI header; the included/excluded ENTRY shape ({type: cell|feature|commit, id, reason?}) is defined in lib/reviews.mjs normalizeScopeEntry — document that entry shape in the skill. (4) The EXISTING specialist wave, severity/synthesis rules, verification-evidence gate, artifact verification, and UAT sections stay contract-identical (goal 5 — do not reduce reviewers or change models) but now run over the SESSION's immutable diff; findings are recorded to the session via record --kind finding, UAT via --kind uat. (5) DELTA RE-REVIEW (R9/A12) — after a P1 fix: re-review the fix delta AND sweep the whole scope diff for the finding's defect class (critical pattern 20260711 grill-deltas); full panel re-run only when the fix crosses a boundary/public contract or destabilizes the scope — then propose the expanded re-review to the user. (6) GATE 4 (R8) — unchanged wording, but it exists ONLY inside a session; P1 blocks session approval (A11); decision recorded via record --kind decision (pending|blocked|approved); gate bypass NEVER creates or auto-approves a review session; no re-dispatch for an approved unchanged range (R6/A7 — bee_reviews.mjs status answers 'reviewed (covered by <id>)'). (7) Lane Scaling table: replace with review-on-demand semantics — the session panel scales to the SCOPE's risk (small scope = 1 correctness reviewer, standard = 4 core, high-risk content = full wave + conditionals); no lane auto-runs any reviewer at feature close; tiny's self-review/done-report stays in bee-swarming solo execution (it is verification, not independent review). Update reviewing-reference.md: finishing checklist closeout no longer sets 'phase reviewing-complete -> Invoke bee-compounding' as a chain stage — session closeout records the decision on the session and restores/leaves feature state untouched (7.5); add a session-record checklist (SPEC §8 fields) and the delta re-review protocol. Keep both files' headings stable where content is unchanged; do not touch any other skill.

truths: the skill's trigger section names explicit user intents only; completion events and merge/ship/release are documented non-triggers with the 7.4 ask-one-question protocol
reviewer dispatch is documented as impossible before a bee_reviews.mjs create succeeds and the scope preview is shown (preflight fail-closed, A10)
the specialist wave / severity / synthesis / artifact verification / UAT contracts remain unreduced (goal 5)
Gate 4 wording exists only within the session flow; bypass never creates or approves sessions (R8)
delta re-review + defect-class sweep is the documented fix protocol; full-panel re-run only on boundary expansion (R9/A12)

artifacts.substantive: user-invoked session protocol: trigger list, scope resolution, freeze+preview via bee_reviews.mjs, unchanged panel contract, delta re-review, session-only Gate 4
session-record checklist (§8 fields), delta re-review protocol, closeout without chain handoff to compounding

key_links: every session mutation the skill instructs goes through node .bee/bin/bee_reviews.mjs verbs shipped in slice 1
docs/history/review-on-demand/SPEC.md §6-§9 wording is the semantic source for trigger/preview/status lines
prohibitions: Do not touch any file outside the two named (swarming/hive/scribing/compounding/AGENTS/docs are cells 5-6)
Do not reduce reviewer count, models, severity rules, or UAT obligations (SPEC §4)
Do not remove the frozen-judge or verification-evidence gate sections

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && ! grep -F "Use when the final swarm slice completes" skills/bee-reviewing/SKILL.md && grep -qF "bee_reviews.mjs" skills/bee-reviewing/SKILL.md

trace.outcome: bee-reviewing rewritten from an automatic post-execution chain stage into a user-invoked review session per SPEC 565e68d0: Trigger section names explicit intents only (merge/ship/release and completion events are documented non-triggers with the 7.4/A9 ask-one-question protocol); Scope Resolution covers the five R4 boundary types with the one-boundary-question rule and batch cumulative-diff mapping; Scope Freeze and Preview wires bee_reviews.mjs create (A10 fail-closed preflight, commit-only-scope caveat) before any dispatch and documents the manifest record; existing specialist wave/severity/synthesis/verification-evidence-gate/artifact-verification/UAT sections kept contract-identical (goal 5) and now record to the session via bee_reviews.mjs record; new Delta Re-Review section (R9/A12) with localized-vs-boundary-crossing fix protocol; Gate 4 rewritten as session-only, gate bypass explicitly never creates/approves sessions (R8), R6/A7 no-re-dispatch rule added; Lane Scaling table replaced with scope-risk-based depth (small/standard/high-risk-content), tiny stays bee-swarming self-review only. reviewing-reference.md gained a Session Record Checklist (SPEC §8 fields) and a Delta Re-Review Protocol section, and the Finishing Checklist/Red Flags no longer chain session closeout into bee-compounding.
trace.verify_output: 206 passed, 0 failed; grep for retired trigger wording found nothing (exit 1 negated to pass); grep for bee_reviews.mjs found matches. Combined command exit 0.
